steps.title: Find the tangent line's slope using perpendicularity
steps.content:
The radius from center $(-1, 1)$ to tangent point $(5, -4)$ has slope
$$\frac{-4 - 1}{5 - (-1)} = \frac{-5}{6}$$
Since the tangent line is perpendicular to the radius, its slope is the negative reciprocal:
$$m_{\text{tangent}} = \frac{6}{5}$$

steps.title: Write the equation of line t
steps.content:
Using point-slope form with point $(5, -4)$ and slope $\frac{6}{5}$:
$$y + 4 = \frac{6}{5}(x - 5)$$
$$y = \frac{6}{5}x - 10$$

steps.title: Backsolve the answer choices
steps.content:
Substitute each choice into $y = \frac{6}{5}x - 10$:

Backsolving — testing choice A $(0, \frac{6}{5})$: $\frac{6}{5} = \frac{6}{5}(0) - 10 = -10$? No. Fails.

Backsolving — testing choice B $(4, 7)$: $7 = \frac{6}{5}(4) - 10 = \frac{24}{5} - 10 = -\frac{26}{5}$? No. Fails.

Backsolving — testing choice C $(10, 2)$: $2 = \frac{6}{5}(10) - 10 = 12 - 10 = 2$? Yes. That matches choice C — C is correct.
